channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Self-Execution Simulation Improves Coding Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.03253v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A promising research direction in enabling LLMs to generate consistently correct code involves addressing their inability to properly estimate program execution, particularly for code they generate. In this work, we demonstrate that Code LLMs can be t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> step-by-step, fine-tuning, LLM, RAG, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Robust LLM Performance Certification via Constrained Maximum Likelihood Estimation
item.description: arXiv:2604.03257v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The ability to rigorously estimate the failure rates of large language models (LLMs) is a prerequisite for their safe deployment. Currently, however, practitioners often face a tradeoff between expensive human gold standards and potentially severely-b...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, LLM, experiment, paper, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SoLA: Leveraging Soft Activation Sparsity and Low-Rank Decomposition for Large Language Model Compression
item.description: arXiv:2604.03258v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities across various tasks, but the billion-scale parameters pose deployment challenges. Although existing methods attempt to reduce the scale of LLMs, they require either special hardwa...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, large language model, LLM, compression, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Why Attend to Everything? Focus is the Key
item.description: arXiv:2604.03260v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Focus, a method that learns which token pairs matter rather than approximating all of them. Learnable centroids assign tokens to groups; distant attention is restricted to same-group pairs while local attention operates at full resolution...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, attention, instruction, vision, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LPC-SM: Local Predictive Coding and Sparse Memory for Long-Context Language Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2604.03263v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most current long-context language models still rely on attention to handle both local interaction and long-range state, which leaves relatively little room to test alternative decompositions of sequence modeling. We propose LPC-SM, a hybrid autoregre...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, memory, context, vision, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Knowledge Packs: Zero-Token Knowledge Delivery via KV Cache Injection
item.description: arXiv:2604.03270v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: RAG wastes tokens. We propose Knowledge Packs: pre-computed KV caches that deliver the same knowledge at zero token cost. For causal transformers, the KV cache from a forward pass on text F is identical to what a joint pass on F+q would produce - this...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, transformer, model, template, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CresOWLve: Benchmarking Creative Problem-Solving Over Real-World Knowledge
item.description: arXiv:2604.03374v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Creative problem-solving requires combining multiple cognitive abilities, including logical reasoning, lateral thinking, analogy-making, and commonsense knowledge, to discover insights that connect seemingly unrelated pieces of information. However, m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, LLM, analysis, model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Noise Steering for Controlled Text Generation: Improving Diversity and Reading-Level Fidelity in Arabic Educational Story Generation
item.description: arXiv:2604.03380v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generating diverse, pedagogically valid stories for Arabic early-grade reading assessments requires balancing tight constraints on vocabulary, reading level, and narrative structure against the need to avoid repetitive plots that undermine assessment ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, transformer, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Toward Full Autonomous Laboratory Instrumentation Control with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.03286v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The control of complex laboratory instrumentation often requires significant programming expertise, creating a barrier for researchers lacking computational skills. This work explores the potential of large language models (LLMs), such as ChatGPT, and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, large language model, LLM, experiment, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating Artificial Intelligence Through a Christian Understanding of Human Flourishing
item.description: arXiv:2604.03356v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) alignment is fundamentally a formation problem, not only a safety problem. As Large Language Models (LLMs) increasingly mediate moral deliberation and spiritual inquiry, they do more than provide information; they function...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, large language model, LLM, framework, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: VERT: Reliable LLM Judges for Radiology Report Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2604.03376v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current literature on radiology report evaluation has focused primarily on designing LLM-based metrics and fine-tuning small models for chest X-rays. However, it remains unclear whether these approaches are robust when applied to reports from other mo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, fine-tuning, LLM, prompt, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hume's Representational Conditions for Causal Judgment: What Bayesian Formalization Abstracted Away
item.description: arXiv:2604.03387v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hume's account of causal judgment presupposes three representational conditions: experiential grounding (ideas must trace to impressions), structured retrieval (association must operate through organized networks exceeding pairwise connection), and vi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, paper, framework, retrieval, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LiME: Lightweight Mixture of Experts for Efficient Multimodal Multi-task Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.02338v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: MoE-PEFT methods combine Mixture of Experts with parameter-efficient fine-tuning for multi-task adaptation, but require separate adapters per expert causing trainable parameters to scale linearly with expert count and limiting applicability to adapter...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, multimodal, experiment, RAG, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SIEVE: Sample-Efficient Parametric Learning from Natural Language
item.description: arXiv:2604.02339v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural language context-such as instructions, knowledge, or feedback-contains rich signal for adapting language models. While in-context learning provides adaptation via the prompt, parametric learning persists into model weights and can improve perf...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, context, in-context, prompt, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Not All Denoising Steps Are Equal: Model Scheduling for Faster Masked Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.02340v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in masked diffusion language models (MDLMs) narrow the quality gap to autoregressive LMs, but their sampling remains expensive because generation requires many full-sequence denoising passes with a large Transformer and, unlike autoreg...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, framework, transformer, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLM Reasoning with Process Rewards for Outcome-Guided Steps
item.description: arXiv:2604.02341v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mathematical reasoning in large language models has improved substantially with reinforcement learning using verifiable rewards, where final answers can be checked automatically and converted into reliable training signals. Most such pipelines optimiz...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, LLM, prompt, RAG, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, prompt engineering, context window, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> audio, framework, chain-of-thought, reasoning, CoT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Cline-Recursive-Chain-of-Thought-System-CRCT- - A framework designed to manage context, dependencies, and tasks in large-scale Cline projects within VS Code

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, retrieval, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> knowledge base, LLM, RAG, framework, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: graphrag - A modular graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.
item.description: SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, RAG, retrieval, API, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, platform, prompt, framework, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Are Arabic Benchmarks Reliable? QIMMA's Quality-First Approach to LLM Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2604.03395v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present QIMMA, a quality-assured Arabic LLM leaderboard that places systematic benchmark validation at its core. Rather than aggregating existing resources as-is, QIMMA applies a multi-model assessment pipeline combining automated LLM judgment with...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, LLM, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: IC3-Evolve: Proof-/Witness-Gated Offline LLM-Driven Heuristic Evolution for IC3 Hardware Model Checking
item.description: arXiv:2604.03232v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: IC3, also known as property-directed reachability (PDR), is a commonly-used algorithm for hardware safety model checking. It checks if a state transition system complies with a given safety property. IC3 either returns UNSAFE (indicating property viol...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, LLM, paper, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Contextual Control without Memory Growth in a Context-Switching Task
item.description: arXiv:2604.03479v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Context-dependent sequential decision making is commonly addressed either by providing context explicitly as an input or by increasing recurrent memory so that contextual information can be represented internally. We study a third alternative: realizi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, study, model, arxiv, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Haiku to Opus in Just 10 bits: LLMs Unlock Massive Compression Gains
item.description: arXiv:2604.02343v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study the compression of LLM-generated text across lossless and lossy regimes, characterizing a compression-compute frontier where more compression is possible at the cost of more compute. For lossless compression, domain-adapted LoRA adapters can ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, compression, prompt, prompting, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Characterizing WebGPU Dispatch Overhead for LLM Inference Across Four GPU Vendors, Three Backends, and Three Browsers
item.description: arXiv:2604.02344v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: WebGPU's security-focused design imposes per-operation validation that compounds across the many small dispatches in neural network inference, yet the true cost of this overhead is poorly characterized. We present a systematic characterization of WebG...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, platform, model, API, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: UI-Oceanus: Scaling GUI Agents with Synthetic Environmental Dynamics
item.description: arXiv:2604.02345v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scaling generalist GUI agents is hindered by the data scalability bottleneck of expensive human demonstrations and the "distillation ceiling" of synthetic teacher supervision. To transcend these limitations, we propose UI-Oceanus, a framework that shi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, RAG, framework, vision, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: fastapi_mcp - Expose your FastAPI endpoints as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, with Auth!

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, model, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: VIGIL: An Extensible System for Real-Time Detection and Mitigation of Cognitive Bias Triggers
item.description: arXiv:2604.03261v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rise of generative AI is posing increasing risks to online information integrity and civic discourse. Most concretely, such risks can materialise in the form of mis- and disinformation. As a mitigation, media-literacy and transparency tools have b...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Position: Science of AI Evaluation Requires Item-level Benchmark Data
item.description: arXiv:2604.03244v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI evaluations have become the primary evidence for deploying generative AI systems across high-stakes domains. However, current evaluation paradigms often exhibit systemic validity failures. These issues, ranging from unjustified design choices to mi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, analysis, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Homophily-aware Supervised Contrastive Counterfactual Augmented Fair Graph Neural Network
item.description: arXiv:2604.02342v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In recent years, Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have achieved remarkable success in tasks such as node classification, link prediction, and graph representation learning. However, they remain susceptible to biases that can arise not only from node attri...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, augmented, framework, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> step-by-step, example, prompt engineering, prompt, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Structural Segmentation of the Minimum Set Cover Problem: Exploiting Universe Decomposability for Metaheuristic Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2604.03234v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Minimum Set Cover Problem (MSCP) is a classical NP-hard combinatorial optimization problem with numerous applications in science and engineering. Although a wide range of exact, approximate, and metaheuristic approaches have been proposed, most me...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: TABQAWORLD: Optimizing Multimodal Reasoning for Multi-Turn Table Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2604.03393v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal reasoning has emerged as a powerful framework for enhancing reasoning capabilities of reasoning models. While multi-turn table reasoning methods have improved reasoning accuracy through tool use and reward modeling, they rely on fixed text ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, framework, model, reasoning, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
